subject: Math

lesson.story_mission: Help the Multiplication Garden crew organize seeds into equal groups, neat arrays, repeated-addition notes, and multiplication equations.
lesson.mini_lesson: Multiplication describes equal groups. You can show the same idea with groups, arrays, repeated addition, and an equation with the × symbol.
lesson.objective: Understand multiplication as equal groups, arrays, repeated addition, and multiplication equations.
lesson.concept_explanation: When every group has the same number of objects, multiplication can tell the total quickly. Rows and columns in an array, repeated addends, and equations such as 3 × 4 = 12 all describe the same total.
lesson.common_misconception: Learners may count unequal groups as multiplication, swap rows and columns without checking the model, add only two numbers, or read the × symbol as addition.
lesson.real_world_connection: Multiplication helps organize classroom supplies, garden trays, teams, snacks, and other real equal-group situations.
lesson.lesson_summary: Check that the groups are equal, name rows and columns, match repeated addition, then write factors and the product in a multiplication equation.
hints: Find the equal groups or rows first.
Count how many are in each group or row.
Connect the model to repeated addition and a multiplication equation.

worked_examples.text: There are 3 equal groups with 4 seeds in each group. Count 4 + 4 + 4 = 12, so 3 × 4 = 12.
An array with 2 rows and 5 columns has 10 counters. The equation is 2 × 5 = 10.
The repeated addition 6 + 6 + 6 + 6 has 4 addends of 6, so it matches 4 × 6 = 24.

misconception_bank.equal_groups_confusion: Learner may count objects without confirming each group has the same amount.
misconception_bank.array_rows_columns_confusion: Learner may swap rows and columns or count only one row or one column.
misconception_bank.repeated_addition_confusion: Learner may add different addends or use the number of addends as the total.
misconception_bank.multiplication_symbol_confusion: Learner may confuse the × symbol with addition or omit the product.

game_theme.title: Multiplication Foundations
game_theme.mission_intro: Organize the Multiplication Garden by proving every equal group, array, repeated addition sentence, and multiplication equation.
